meta.title: Partial Invoices: How to Request Deposits and Progress Payments
meta.description: Learn how to use partial invoices to secure upfront deposits, bill for project milestones, and protect your cash flow on long-term projects.

schema.headline: Partial Invoices: How to Request Deposits

blocks.title: Partial Invoices: Securing Deposits & Progress Payments
blocks.subtitle: Never fund a client's project out of your own pocket. Learn how to use partial invoices to collect upfront deposits and milestone payments safely.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: Protecting Your Cash Flow
blocks.text: For massive projects—like building a house, designing a website, or consulting over six months—waiting until the very end to send a single invoice is a recipe for bankruptcy. A partial invoice (also known as a deposit invoice or progress bill) allows you to break a massive contract down into smaller, manageable chunks that you bill for along the way.

events.title: The Deposit
events.description: You issue a Partial Invoice for 30% of the total quote upfront before beginning any work.

events.title: Progress Payment
events.description: Halfway through the project, you issue a second Partial Invoice for the next 30%.

events.title: The Final Invoice
events.description: Upon project completion, you issue the Final Invoice for the remaining 40%, referencing the previous payments.

steps.title: Reference the Total Project Cost
steps.description: Always remind the client of the big picture. State 'Total Project Quote: $10,000' at the top of the invoice.

steps.title: Bill the Specific Fraction
steps.description: Add a line item that clearly explains the math: '50% Upfront Deposit for Web Design Services - $5,000'.

steps.title: Provide a Receipt
steps.description: Once the partial invoice is paid, send a receipt so the client has proof they've paid off that specific milestone.

blocks.tip: For large material-heavy jobs (like construction), always ensure your first partial invoice covers 100% of the raw material costs. Only finance your labor, never finance physical goods.

questions.question: Does a partial invoice need its own invoice number?
questions.description: Yes. Every time you demand money, it must be on a document with a unique, sequential invoice number for your tax records. Do not just resend the original estimate with a note.

blocks.heading: Need a deposit to start working?
blocks.subtext: Change the title of our free generator to 'Deposit Invoice' and secure your cash flow today.
